text: Technical debt (Ward Cunningham, 1992) is the implied cost of future rework caused by choosing an expedient solution now instead of a better approach that would take longer. Like financial debt, it accrues interest — the longer you delay, the more it costs to fix.
Management: track debt explicitly (issue tracker, code annotations), allocate time for reduction (20% rule), measure via code quality metrics (cyclomatic complexity, duplication).
